era_context: Same as -1029: Late Bronze Age collapse aftermath, early Iron Age consolidation. Egypt under Ramesses XI; Phoenician city-states rising; Aegean Geometric period; Mesopotamian weakness; Vedic period in South Asia.

events.title: Continued priestly power consolidation at Thebes, Egypt
events.description: During the reign of Ramesses XI, the High Priests of Amun at Thebes continue to accumulate wealth and administrative authority over Upper Egypt. By the late 11th century BCE (within which -1028 falls), the priesthood controls vast temple estates, commands significant military resources, and effectively governs Upper Egypt independently of the pharaoh. This process, documented in administrative papyri and temple inscriptions, represents a fundamental shift in Egyptian political structure and foreshadows the division of Egypt in the Third Intermediate Period.
key_figures: High Priests of Amun (names vary; specific individuals for -1028 not identified)

sources.name: Papyrus Harris I and other administrative papyri from the late Ramessid period
Temple inscriptions from Karnak and Luxor

events.certainty_note: The general process is well-documented; specific events for -1028 are not attested.

disconfirming_evidence: No disconfirming evidence identified for events listed.
historiographic_note: Documentation for -1028 is essentially non-existent at the year-specific level. We rely on longer-term administrative and archaeological records. The record is heavily Egypt-centric and skewed toward elite (pharaonic and priestly) perspectives.
